text: The best among you are those who have the best manners and character.
None of you truly believes until he wishes for his brother what he wishes for himself.
The strong man is not the one who can overpower others. The strong man is the one who controls himself when he is angry.
Make things easy and do not make them difficult. Give good tidings and do not drive people away.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him speak good or remain silent.
The best of people are those who are most beneficial to people.
A Muslim is the one from whose tongue and hands other Muslims are safe.
Allah does not look at your bodies or your forms, but He looks at your hearts and your deeds.
Smiling in the face of your brother is an act of charity.
Be in this world as if you were a stranger or a traveler.
Richness is not having many possessions. Rather, true richness is the richness of the soul.
The believer does not taunt others, nor curse others, nor is he immoral or shameless.
Kindness is a mark of faith, and whoever is not kind has no faith.
Every act of kindness is charity.
Do not belittle any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.
The most beloved of deeds to Allah are those that are most consistent, even if they are small.
Whoever is not grateful to people, is not grateful to Allah.
He who eats his fill while his neighbor goes without food is not a believer.
When you see a person who has been given more than you in money and beauty, look to those who have been given less.

Take advantage of five before five: your youth before your old age, your health before your sickness, your wealth before your poverty, your free time before your busyness, and your life before your death.
The most perfect of the believers in faith is the one who is best in character.
Modesty is part of faith.
Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.
Leave that which makes you doubt for that which does not make you doubt.
Feed the hungry, visit the sick, and set free the captives.
A good word is charity.
The ink of the scholar is holier than the blood of the martyr.
He is not one of us who does not have mercy on our young and does not respect our elders.
The world is a prison for the believer and a paradise for the disbeliever.
Verily, Allah is beautiful and He loves beauty.
The best charity is that given in Ramadan.
Whoever builds a mosque for Allah, Allah will build for him a house in Paradise.

The two feet of a servant will not move on the Day of Judgment until he is asked about his life and how he spent it, his knowledge and what he did with it, his wealth and how he earned and spent it, and his body and how he used it.
Allah will shade seven types of people under His shade on the Day when there will be no shade but His.
The supplication is the essence of worship.
Paradise lies at the feet of your mother.
Cleanliness is half of faith.
Whoever treads a path in search of knowledge, Allah will make easy for him the path to Paradise.
The one who looks after a widow or a poor person is like a warrior who fights for Allah's cause.
Beware of envy, for envy consumes good deeds just as fire consumes wood.
No fatigue, nor disease, nor sorrow, nor sadness, nor hurt, nor distress befalls a Muslim, even if it were the prick he receives from a thorn, but that Allah expiates some of his sins for that.
Whoever removes a worldly hardship from a believer, Allah will remove from him one of the hardships of the Day of Resurrection.
The best of you are those who learn the Quran and teach it.
Part of the perfection of a person's Islam is his leaving that which does not concern him.
Speak the truth even if it is bitter.
Allah is gentle and loves gentleness in all things.
The best form of worship is awaiting relief with patience.
Whoever has an atom's weight of arrogance in his heart will not enter Paradise.
The believer is not stung from the same hole twice.
Give the laborer his wages before his sweat dries.
The merciful are shown mercy by the Most Merciful. Show mercy to those on earth, and the One above the heavens will show mercy to you.
When a man dies, his deeds come to an end except for three things: ongoing charity, knowledge which is beneficial, or a righteous child who prays for him.
Tie your camel and then put your trust in Allah.
The best of houses is a house where an orphan is treated well.
He who is deprived of gentleness is deprived of all good.
Do not waste water even if you are at a running stream.
The believers, in their mutual kindness, compassion, and sympathy, are just like one body. When one limb suffers, the whole body responds with sleeplessness and fever.
Actions are judged by intentions, and every person will be rewarded according to their intention.
Whoever is patient, Allah will give him patience, and no one is given a gift better and more comprehensive than patience.
There is a reward for kindness to every living thing.
The best of you is the one who is best to his wife, and I am the best of you to my wives.
Whoever does not show mercy to the people, Allah will not show mercy to him.
The upper hand is better than the lower hand. The upper hand is the one that gives, and the lower hand is the one that receives.
Fear Allah wherever you are, follow a bad deed with a good deed and it will erase it, and treat people with good character.
Whoever guides someone to goodness will have a reward like the one who did it.
The most hated speech to Allah is when a man says to another man, 'Fear Allah!' and he replies, 'Mind your own business.'
Verily, with hardship comes ease.
A woman entered the Hellfire because of a cat which she tied up. She neither fed it nor let it eat from the vermin of the earth.
Allah does not judge you according to your appearance and your wealth, but He looks at your hearts and your actions.
Charity does not decrease wealth.

The best supplication on the Day of Arafah, and the best thing that I and the Prophets before me said, is: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner, to Him belongs the dominion, to Him belongs all praise, and He has power over all things.
Whoever fasts Ramadan out of faith and seeking reward, his previous sins will be forgiven.
Whoever stands in prayer during Laylat al-Qadr out of faith and seeking reward, his previous sins will be forgiven.
The one who maintains family ties is not the one who reciprocates. Rather, the one who maintains family ties is the one who restores them when they are cut off.
Allah will not be merciful to those who are not merciful to people.
The best jihad is a word of truth spoken to an unjust ruler.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him honor his neighbor.
Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him honor his guest.
No one will enter Paradise who has even a mustard seed's weight of arrogance in his heart, and no one will enter the Fire who has even a mustard seed's weight of faith in his heart.
The prayer is a light, charity is a proof, and patience is illumination.

Do not be people without minds of your own, saying that if others treat you well you will treat them well, and that if they do wrong you will do wrong. Instead, accustom yourselves to do good if people do good and not to do wrong if they do evil.
A believer to another believer is like a building whose different parts enforce each other.
The most complete of the believers in faith is the one with the best character among them, and the best of you are the best to your women.
There is no disease that Allah has created except that He also has created its cure.
Make use of medical treatment, for Allah has not created a disease without creating a cure for it, except for one disease — old age.
The son of Adam does not fill any vessel worse than his stomach. It is sufficient for the son of Adam to eat a few morsels to keep him upright.
Exchange gifts, for this will increase your love for one another.
Whoever is slow to act, his lineage will not make him faster.
The best remembrance is: There is no god but Allah. And the best supplication is: All praise is due to Allah.
He who does not thank people does not thank Allah.
Whoever covers the faults of a Muslim, Allah will cover his faults on the Day of Resurrection.
Do not hate each other, do not envy each other, do not turn away from each other, but be servants of Allah as brothers.
Every good deed is charity. Among the good deeds is to meet your brother with a smiling face and to pour water from your bucket into your brother's vessel.
The one who recites the Quran and is proficient in it will be with the noble and righteous scribes. The one who recites the Quran and falters in it, finding it difficult, will have a double reward.
Whoever relieves a believer of a distress of this world, Allah will relieve him of a distress of the Day of Resurrection.
The two rak'ahs before Fajr are better than the world and everything in it.
If any one of you improves his practice of Islam, then every good deed he does will be rewarded tenfold to seven hundred times its value.
A person will be with those whom he loves.
The best prayer after the obligatory prayers is the night prayer.
Whoever prays the two cool prayers will enter Paradise.
Verily, truthfulness leads to righteousness, and righteousness leads to Paradise.
Beware of lying, for lying leads to wickedness, and wickedness leads to the Hellfire.
It is enough of a sin for a person to neglect those he is responsible for.
The best of deeds is the prayer at its proper time.
Modesty brings nothing but good.
The most beloved of places to Allah are the mosques, and the most hated of places to Allah are the markets.
Whoever says 'SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi' one hundred times a day, his sins will be forgiven even if they were like the foam of the sea.
When the son of Adam dies, his record of deeds is closed except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, and a righteous child who prays for him.
The best of companions in the sight of Allah is the best of them to his companion, and the best of neighbors is the best of them to his neighbor.
Shall I not tell you something that is better than fasting, prayer, and charity? It is reconciling between people.
A man asked the Prophet: What is the best thing in Islam? He said: Feeding others and giving the greeting of peace to those you know and those you do not know.
There is no sin more deserving of having its punishment hastened in this world, in addition to what is stored up for the Hereafter, than oppression and severing family ties.
A Muslim who plants a tree or sows a field, and a human being or an animal eats from it, it shall be reckoned as charity for him.
The nearest a servant comes to his Lord is when he is prostrating, so make much supplication in prostration.
Spread peace, feed the hungry, maintain the ties of kinship, and pray at night while people are sleeping, and you will enter Paradise in peace.
Your body has a right over you, your eyes have a right over you, and your wife has a right over you.
He who cheats us is not one of us.
Do not argue with your brother, do not joke with him excessively, and do not make a promise to him that you cannot keep.
By Allah, he does not believe! By Allah, he does not believe! By Allah, he does not believe! It was said: Who, O Messenger of Allah? He said: The one whose neighbor is not safe from his evil.
Fasting is a shield. When any one of you is fasting, he should neither behave in an obscene manner nor argue. If someone fights him or insults him, let him say: I am fasting.
The best fast after Ramadan is in the month of Muharram.
The best of marriages are those that are easiest.
No man should be alone with a woman, and no woman should travel except with a mahram.
If you were to rely upon Allah with the reliance He is due, you would be provided for as the birds are provided for: they go out in the morning hungry and return full.
Dua is worship.
Nothing can change the divine decree except supplication.
Your Lord is generous and shy. If His servant raises his hands to Him in supplication, He is shy to return them empty.
When Allah loves a servant, He tests him.

Wonderful is the affair of the believer, for his affairs are all good. If something good happens to him, he is thankful and that is good for him. If something bad happens to him, he is patient and that is good for him.
The best among you is the one who pays back his debts in the best manner.
Whoever guarantees me what is between his two jaw-bones and what is between his two legs, I guarantee him Paradise.
Guard yourselves against the Fire, even if it is with half a date given in charity.

Both parties in a business transaction have the right to annul it as long as they have not separated. If they are honest and disclose any defects, their transaction will be blessed. If they lie and conceal defects, the blessing will be erased.
Allah has forbidden you to be undutiful to your mothers, to withhold what you should give, to demand what you do not deserve, and to bury your daughters alive.
Do not turn away a poor man even if all you can give is half a date. If you love the poor and bring them near you, Allah will bring you near Him on the Day of Resurrection.
Removing a harmful thing from the road is charity.
I and the one who sponsors an orphan will be like these two in Paradise — and he indicated his index and middle fingers.
Whoever builds a mosque for Allah, even if it is as small as a bird's nest, Allah will build for him a house in Paradise.
If the Final Hour comes while you have a seedling in your hand, and you are able to plant it before the Hour comes, then plant it.
There are two blessings that many people are deceived into losing: health and free time.
Allah is pleased with His servant who praises Him when he eats something and praises Him when he drinks something.
Three supplications are answered without doubt: the supplication of the oppressed, the supplication of the traveler, and the supplication of the parent for his child.

The rights of a Muslim upon another Muslim are six: when you meet him, greet him; when he invites you, accept; when he seeks your counsel, advise him; when he sneezes and praises Allah, say Yarhamuk-Allah; when he is sick, visit him; when he dies, follow his funeral.
Whoever does not give up false speech and acting upon it, Allah has no need for him to give up his food and drink.
Three things follow a dead person to his grave, two of which return and one remains: his family, his wealth, and his deeds follow him. His family and wealth return, but his deeds remain.
Remember frequently the destroyer of pleasures — death.
The grave is the first stage of the Hereafter. Whoever is saved from it, whatever comes after it is easier. And whoever is not saved from it, whatever comes after it is worse.
Each of you is a shepherd and each of you is responsible for his flock.
Whoever takes a path to gain knowledge, Allah will make the path to Paradise easy for him.
Allah has revealed to me that you must be humble so that no one oppresses another or boasts over another.
Help your brother whether he is an oppressor or is oppressed. If he is the oppressor, restrain him, for that is helping him.
There should be no harm and no reciprocal harm.

Avoid suspicion, for suspicion is the most false of speech. Do not spy on one another, do not compete with one another, do not envy one another, do not hate one another, and do not turn away from one another. Be servants of Allah, as brothers.
Every Muslim has to give in charity. If he cannot find anything to give, let him work so that he can benefit himself and give in charity.
Religion is sincerity. We asked: To whom? He said: To Allah, His Book, His Messenger, the leaders of the Muslims, and their common people.

No one of you should wish for death because of a misfortune that befalls him. If he must wish for it, let him say: O Allah, keep me alive as long as life is better for me, and cause me to die when death is better for me.
The best of you is the one who learns the Quran and teaches it.

A man came to the Prophet and said: O Messenger of Allah, who is most deserving of my good companionship? He said: Your mother. He asked: Then who? He said: Your mother. He asked: Then who? He said: Your mother. He asked: Then who? He said: Your father.
Allah does not look at your figures nor your attire but He looks at your hearts and your deeds.
If anyone travels on a road in search of knowledge, Allah will cause him to travel on one of the roads of Paradise.
Beware of injustice, for injustice will be darkness on the Day of Resurrection.
The one who looks after a widow or a needy person is like one who strives in the way of Allah, or like one who prays all night without rest and fasts without breaking his fast.
The best among you are those who are best in paying off their debts.
Whoever eats from this plant — meaning garlic — let him not approach our mosque, for the angels are harmed by what harms the children of Adam.
There are no days on which righteous deeds are more beloved to Allah than the first ten days of Dhul Hijjah.
The best of charity is that given by one who has little. The upper hand is better than the lower hand, and start with those for whom you are responsible.
If a Muslim plants a tree or cultivates a field, and a bird, a human being, or an animal eats from it, it is regarded as a charitable deed for him.
Whoever gives respite to a debtor who is in difficulty or forgives him, Allah will shade him under His Throne on the Day of Resurrection.
The best day on which the sun has risen is Friday: on it Adam was created, on it he was admitted to Paradise, and on it he was expelled from it.
Hasten to do good deeds before you are overtaken by trials that are like portions of a dark night.
Allah the Most High said: I am as My servant thinks of Me. I am with him when he remembers Me. If he remembers Me in himself, I remember him in Myself.
He who sees something hateful should change it with his hand. If he is unable, then with his tongue. If he is unable, then with his heart, and that is the weakest of faith.
The Hour will not come until knowledge is taken away, earthquakes increase, time passes quickly, tribulations arise, and killing increases.
Whoever acquires knowledge that should be sought for the sake of Allah, only to get some worldly gain, will not smell the fragrance of Paradise on the Day of Resurrection.
The best of you are those who are best to their families, and I am the best of you to my family.

Shall I not tell you about the inhabitants of Paradise? Every humble, gentle person who, if he were to swear by Allah, would fulfill his oath. Shall I not tell you about the inhabitants of the Fire? Every harsh, arrogant, and proud person.
Verily, Allah has prescribed excellence in all things.
Make things easy and do not make them difficult, cheer people up and do not drive them away.
There is no prayer for one who does not recite the Opening of the Book.
Two things the people will never be thankful enough for: their health and their free time.
The gates of Paradise are opened on Monday and Thursday, and every servant who does not associate anything with Allah is forgiven, except a man who has a grudge against his brother.
The Muslim does not inherit from the disbeliever and the disbeliever does not inherit from the Muslim.
He who is an expert in reciting the Quran is with the honorable, righteous scribes, and he who reads the Quran and finds it difficult, stammering over its verses, will have a double reward.
Say 'I believe in Allah' and then be steadfast.
The best of actions is to love for the sake of Allah and to hate for the sake of Allah.

Do you know who the bankrupt person is? The bankrupt of my nation is the one who comes on the Day of Resurrection with prayers, fasting, and charity, but he had insulted, slandered, consumed the wealth of others, shed blood, and beaten others.
The most beloved of speech to Allah is when the servant says: Glory be to You, O Allah, and with Your praise. Blessed is Your name and exalted is Your majesty. There is no god but You.

Whoever asks Allah for Paradise three times, Paradise says: O Allah, admit him into Paradise. And whoever seeks protection from the Fire three times, the Fire says: O Allah, protect him from the Fire.
Teach and make things easy, not difficult. When one of you is angry, let him be silent.
The Muslim's prayer in congregation is twenty-five degrees more excellent than his prayer in his house or in his shop.
Eat together and do not eat separately, for the blessing is in the community.
Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every obligatory prayer, nothing will prevent him from entering Paradise except death.
The one who is proficient in the Quran will be with the noble righteous scribes in Heaven, and the one who reads it and finds it difficult will have two rewards.
Wealth is not diminished by giving in charity. Allah raises the honor of one who forgives, and one who shows humility for the sake of Allah, Allah raises him in status.
Allah does not burden a soul beyond that it can bear.
None of you should drink while standing. If someone forgets, let him vomit.
The most truthful of speech is the Book of Allah, the best of guidance is the guidance of Muhammad, and the worst of matters are newly invented ones.
Keep to truthfulness, for truthfulness leads to righteousness, and righteousness leads to Paradise.
The most beloved of names to Allah are Abdullah and Abdur-Rahman.

Whoever makes the Hereafter his goal, Allah gathers his affairs for him, gives him richness of heart, and the world comes to him willingly. And whoever makes the world his goal, Allah scatters his affairs, places poverty between his eyes, and nothing of the world comes to him except what has been decreed for him.
